chapter_summary: At the pool of Bethesda, Jesus heals a man who has been ill for 38 years (just with a word), on the Sabbath. The Jewish leaders question the man and then confront Jesus. Jesus responds with a profound teaching: the Father and Son work together; the Son gives life; the hour is coming when the dead will hear the Son's voice; those who believe have already passed from death to life. Jesus then calls four witnesses to his identity: John the Baptist, His works, the Father's voice, and the Scriptures themselves.
main_lesson: Jesus healed a man who had been sick for 38 years — with just His word. He is Lord over sickness, over the Sabbath, and over life and death itself. The Father gave everything to the Son.

memory_verse.kjv: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that heareth my word, and believeth on him that sent me, hath everlasting life, and shall not come into condemnation; but is passed from death unto life.
memory_verse.little_bible: Jesus said: 'I'm telling you the truth — whoever hears what I say and trusts in the Father who sent Me already has eternal life. They have already crossed over from death into life!'

parent_guide: John 5 contains some of the most important Christological statements in the Gospels. Jesus claims equality with God (v18), life-giving authority (v21), judgment authority (v22), and to be the source of resurrection (v28-29). The healing itself is brief — only 9 verses — but the teaching it provokes fills the rest of the chapter. For children, the healing story is accessible and exciting; the theological teaching (vv19-47) can be introduced simply: 'Jesus was explaining to the leaders who He really is — God's own Son, equal to God.' v24 is one of the clearest statements of the gospel in the NT.
application_for_children: We can trust what Jesus says. When He speaks, amazing things happen — even sick people get well and people who were spiritually dead come alive. His words are powerful and true.

verses.kjv: After this there was a feast of the Jews; and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
verses.little_bible: After this, there was a special Jewish celebration, and Jesus went to Jerusalem.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus went to Jerusalem for a big celebration.
verses.meaning: Jesus attended the Jewish feasts, fulfilling the law and connecting with His people. Jerusalem was the centre of Jewish worship and would be the scene of growing conflict.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus went to Jerusalem.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for always going where Your Father calls You. Help me to go where You lead.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus went to the Jewish feasts? What does that tell us about Him?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus participated in His people's celebrations and traditions. How does your family honour God in special celebrations or traditions?
verses.do_it_today: Find one way to honour God in something you do today — even in something ordinary.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus and His disciples walking through a bustling Jerusalem street during a festival — colourful banners, market stalls, crowds of people celebrating.

verses.kjv: Now there is at Jerusalem by the sheep market a pool, which is called in the Hebrew tongue Bethesda, having five porches.
verses.little_bible: Near the Sheep Gate in Jerusalem was a pool called Bethesda. It had five covered porches around it.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Near a gate in Jerusalem was a special pool called Bethesda.
verses.meaning: Bethesda means 'house of mercy' or 'house of grace.' The pool was a real place, later confirmed by archaeology. Its five porches provided shelter for the many sick people who gathered there.
verses.memory_phrase: A pool called Bethesda.
verses.prayer: God, You are a God of mercy. Thank You for bringing help to those who have nowhere else to go.
verses.discussion_question: The name Bethesda means 'house of mercy.' Why is that a fitting name for a place where Jesus showed up?
verses.family_discussion: Many sick and helpless people gathered at Bethesda. Are there places in your community where people gather because they have no other hope? How can your family be part of God's mercy to them?
verses.do_it_today: Show mercy to someone today — someone who may not deserve it or may not be able to pay you back.
verses.illustration_prompt: A large stone pool with five covered porches in ancient Jerusalem, surrounded by sick and disabled people lying on mats. The water still and blue. Gentle afternoon light.

verses.kjv: In these lay a great multitude of impotent folk, of blind, halt, withered, waiting for the moving of the water.
verses.little_bible: Crowds of sick people lay in those porches — blind people, people who could not walk, and people with weak and wasting bodies. They were all waiting for the water to move.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Many sick people lay there, waiting for the water to move.
verses.meaning: The porches were crowded with human suffering. 'Impotent' means lacking strength; 'halt' means lame. The scene sets up the depth of need that Jesus was about to walk into.
verses.memory_phrase: Many sick people were waiting.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You see all the sick and hurting people in the world. Thank You that You care about each one.
verses.discussion_question: Imagine lying there sick for a long time, just waiting and hoping. How would you feel? What would you need?
verses.family_discussion: The world has many hurting, helpless people waiting for help. How does God call us to respond to them?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for someone you know who is sick or hurting — name them out loud to God today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Rows of sick and disabled people resting on mats under stone porches, some blind, some with withered limbs. A sense of quiet, patient suffering and waiting.

verses.kjv: For an angel went down at a certain season into the pool, and troubled the water: whosoever then first after the troubling of the water stepped in was made whole of whatsoever disease he had.
verses.little_bible: People believed that at certain times an angel would stir the water in the pool, and the first person to jump in after the stirring would be healed of whatever was wrong with them.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: People believed the first person into the moving water would be healed.
verses.meaning: This was the popular belief about the pool — whether miraculous or natural, people came hoping for healing. The competition made it nearly impossible for the weakest to ever get in first.
verses.memory_phrase: The first one in would be healed.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that Your healing is not a competition. You give grace freely to all who come to Jesus.
verses.discussion_question: Only the first person in could be healed. What was unfair about that? Who would never get a chance?
verses.family_discussion: In this world, those with strength and connections often get help first. How does Jesus turn that upside down?
verses.do_it_today: Look for someone who is always last in line or left out. Do something kind for them today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A crowd of sick people pressing toward the edge of the pool, the water rippling and shimmering with a faint heavenly glow, the weakest ones unable to reach it.

verses.kjv: And a certain man was there, which had an infirmity thirty and eight years.
verses.little_bible: One man there had been sick for thirty-eight years — almost his whole life.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: One man had been sick for 38 whole years.
verses.meaning: 38 years is a lifetime of suffering. The number echoes Israel's 38 years of wandering in the wilderness. This man had not known a healthy day in longer than most people in the crowd had been alive.
verses.memory_phrase: Sick for thirty-eight years.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You see people who have been hurting for a very long time. Please bring Your healing to them.
verses.discussion_question: Can you imagine being sick for 38 years? How do you think that man felt about his life?
verses.family_discussion: Some people wait a very long time for God to act. How can we encourage people who have been waiting a long time for healing or help?
verses.do_it_today: Write a note or draw a picture for someone who has been going through a hard time for a long time.
verses.illustration_prompt: An older man lying on a worn mat by the pool, with the marks of long illness on his face but quiet endurance in his eyes. He has been there longer than anyone.

verses.kjv: When Jesus saw him lie, and knew that he had been now a long time in that case, he saith unto him, Wilt thou be made whole?
verses.little_bible: Jesus saw the man lying there and knew he had been sick for a very long time. Jesus asked him, 'Would you like to get well?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus saw him and asked, 'Do you want to get better?'
verses.meaning: Jesus singled out this one man from a crowd of sick people. He knew the man's whole story. His question is not ignorant — it invites the man to express his desire and engage his will.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus asked, 'Do you want to be well?'
verses.prayer: Jesus, You see me and You know my story. I do want to be well — in my body and in my heart.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus asked 'Do you want to be made well?' when it was obvious the man was sick?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus personally notices each person's need. How does it feel to know that Jesus sees you — individually — and cares about your specific situation?
verses.do_it_today: Ask someone in your family today: 'How are you really doing?' and truly listen to their answer.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus kneeling down to the level of the man on his mat, looking him directly in the eyes with warmth and purpose. The crowd around them fades as this moment becomes intimate.

verses.kjv: The impotent man answered him, Sir, I have no man, when the water is troubled, to put me into the pool: but while I am coming, another steppeth down before me.
verses.little_bible: The sick man said, 'Sir, I have no one to help me into the pool when the water moves. Every time I try to get in, someone else gets there before me.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The man said, 'I have no one to help me get into the water.'
verses.meaning: The man answers with his problem, not his hope. He is not even asking Jesus for healing — he is explaining why healing has been impossible. He has no helper, no advocate, no friend. His answer reveals deep loneliness alongside his illness.
verses.memory_phrase: I have no one to help me.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You are the helper for people who have no one. You are my helper.
verses.discussion_question: The man explained why he couldn't get better instead of asking Jesus for help. Do we sometimes explain our problems instead of asking God for help?
verses.family_discussion: The man had been alone in his struggle for 38 years. Who in your community might be going through something hard all alone? How can your family be a helper?
verses.do_it_today: Be a helper for someone today — look for a person who seems to be struggling alone and offer to help.
verses.illustration_prompt: The man on his mat gesturing toward the pool, explaining his helplessness to Jesus with tired, weary eyes — the water visible in the background with others getting in ahead of him.

verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto him, Rise, take up thy bed, and walk.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said to him, 'Get up! Pick up your mat and walk!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'Get up, pick up your mat, and walk!'
verses.meaning: Jesus doesn't wait for the water to be stirred. He doesn't touch the man. He simply speaks — and His word is powerful enough to do what 38 years of waiting could not. The command to take up the bed and walk signals complete healing.
verses.memory_phrase: Rise, take up your bed and walk!
verses.prayer: Jesus, Your words have power. Speak to the things in my life that need Your healing touch.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus didn't use the pool at all — He just spoke. What does that tell us about how powerful His words are?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus' word alone was enough to heal 38 years of sickness. Are there areas of your life where you need to trust that God's word is enough — even without seeing how it could happen?
verses.do_it_today: Read one of Jesus' commands in the Bible out loud today. Talk about what it would mean to obey it — and take one step to do it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing tall, pointing upward as He speaks the command with authority. The man's eyes wide with astonishment as life surges back into his body.

verses.kjv: And immediately the man was made whole, and took up his bed, and walked: and on the same day was the sabbath.
verses.little_bible: Right away the man was completely healed! He picked up his mat and walked — and all of this happened on the Sabbath, the Jewish day of rest.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Right away the man was healed! He picked up his mat and walked.
verses.meaning: The healing was instant and complete. The detail that it was the Sabbath sets up the conflict that follows. Jesus knowingly healed on the Sabbath — it was not an accident. He was making a point about His authority.
verses.memory_phrase: Immediately the man was healed.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You can heal completely and instantly. I trust Your power and Your perfect timing.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think John tells us it was the Sabbath? Do you think Jesus knew what day it was when He healed the man?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus healed on the Sabbath on purpose. He wanted to show He is Lord even over the Sabbath. What rules have people invented that Jesus might want to challenge today?
verses.do_it_today: Celebrate something God has done — take a moment to tell someone about a time God helped or healed in your family.
verses.illustration_prompt: The man leaping to his feet, holding his mat above his head in joy, tears streaming down his face. People around him staring in astonishment. Sunlight flooding the scene.

verses.kjv: The Jews therefore said unto him that was cured, It is the sabbath day: it is not lawful for thee to carry thy bed.
verses.little_bible: Some Jewish leaders saw the man carrying his mat and told him, 'It's the Sabbath! You're not allowed to carry your bed today!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The leaders said, 'You cannot carry your mat on the Sabbath!'
verses.meaning: The leaders noticed the healed man before they noticed the miracle. Their concern was rule-keeping, not rejoicing. They were focused on a man carrying a mat rather than celebrating that a man who had been sick 38 years was now walking.
verses.memory_phrase: The leaders cared more about rules than the miracle.
verses.prayer: God, help me to celebrate what You do, not just follow rules. Help me to see Your works with joy.
verses.discussion_question: The leaders saw a miracle happen and talked about rules instead of celebrating. Why do you think they did that?
verses.family_discussion: It's easy to miss the big, wonderful thing God is doing because we are focused on small rules. How can we train ourselves to notice and celebrate what God is doing?
verses.do_it_today: Look for something good that God is doing in or around your family today — and celebrate it out loud.
verses.illustration_prompt: Stern-faced Jewish leaders in long robes pointing accusingly at the joyful healed man holding his mat. He looks surprised — he is carrying the proof of his miracle.

verses.kjv: He answered them, He that made me whole, the same said unto me, Take up thy bed, and walk.
verses.little_bible: The man said, 'The one who healed me told me to pick up my mat and walk!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The man said, 'The one who healed me told me to pick it up!'
verses.meaning: The healed man points straight back to his healer. He is simply obeying the one who healed him. His answer is perfectly logical — the one with power to heal has authority to give instructions.
verses.memory_phrase: I'm obeying the one who healed me.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when I obey You, help me to do it confidently — knowing You have the authority to tell me what to do.
verses.discussion_question: The man said he was just doing what the person who healed him told him. Is that a good answer? Why or why not?
verses.family_discussion: When Jesus gives us instructions, do we follow them even when others question us? How can we help each other be brave enough to obey Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Is there something Jesus has told you to do that you've been putting off? Take one step to obey Him today.
verses.illustration_prompt: The healed man standing tall and confident, gesturing back toward where Jesus had been, explaining himself to the frowning leaders with simple, clear conviction.

verses.kjv: Then asked they him, What man is that which said unto thee, Take up thy bed, and walk?
verses.little_bible: The leaders asked him, 'Who is this man who told you to pick up your mat and walk?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The leaders asked, 'Who told you to pick it up?'
verses.meaning: The leaders' question reveals their priorities. They are not asking 'Who healed you?' or 'How do you feel after 38 years?' They are looking for someone to blame for breaking the Sabbath rules.
verses.memory_phrase: Who told you to do this?
verses.prayer: God, help me to care most about the right things — not just about rules, but about Your love and power.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the leaders asked 'who told you?' instead of 'who healed you?' What were they really after?
verses.family_discussion: The leaders were looking for fault instead of celebrating healing. Can we sometimes do the same thing — find fault with how God moves instead of rejoicing? How do we guard against that?
verses.do_it_today: When something good happens today, choose to focus on the good — not on finding something to criticise.
verses.illustration_prompt: The leaders pressing in close to the healed man with suspicious, narrowed eyes, their robes and scrolls marking them as authority figures. Their question has an edge of accusation to it.

verses.kjv: And he that was healed wist not who it was: for Jesus had conveyed himself away, a multitude being in that place.
verses.little_bible: The healed man didn't even know who Jesus was — Jesus had quietly slipped away into the crowd!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The man didn't know Jesus' name. Jesus had slipped away into the crowd.
verses.meaning: Jesus healed without seeking recognition or credit. He performed one of the most remarkable miracles in His ministry and then quietly disappeared. This is humility in action — serving without needing the spotlight.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus helped quietly and slipped away.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me to help people without needing everyone to notice. Teach me Your humble heart.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus healed the man and then walked away without telling him His name. What does that show you about Jesus' character?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus gave a great gift without waiting to be thanked or recognised. How can your family practice serving others without expecting credit?
verses.do_it_today: Do one kind thing today without telling anyone — a secret act of kindness, just for God to see.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus quietly blending into a moving crowd, head slightly bowed, while the healed man stands at a distance still holding his mat — they are now separated by many people.

verses.kjv: Afterward Jesus findeth him in the temple, and said unto him, Behold, thou art made whole: sin no more, lest a worse thing come unto thee.
verses.little_bible: Later, Jesus found the man in the Temple and said, 'Look — you are well now! Stop sinning, or something even worse might happen to you.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus found the man later and said, 'You are well! Stop sinning.'
verses.meaning: Jesus sought the man out again — this time in the Temple. Physical healing alone is not the end goal; Jesus cares about the whole person. His warning about sin is not cruel but deeply caring — He wants the man to have lasting wholeness, not just temporary health.
verses.memory_phrase: Be healed — and sin no more.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for healing me and for caring about my whole life — my body and my heart. Help me to sin no more.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus healed the man's body but also talked about his soul. Why do you think both mattered to Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus is interested in our whole lives — not just our bodies or our circumstances, but our hearts and choices. How can we invite Jesus into every part of our lives?
verses.do_it_today: Talk honestly to God about one area of your life where you need His help to do better — not just to feel better.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus finding the man inside the beautiful Temple courts, speaking to him quietly and seriously with a hand on his shoulder. The man's face shows both relief and solemn attention.

verses.kjv: The man departed, and told the Jews that it was Jesus, which had made him whole.
verses.little_bible: The man went and told the Jewish leaders that it was Jesus who had healed him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The man told the leaders: 'Jesus healed me!'
verses.meaning: The man went and told the authorities it was Jesus. Some see this as an act of gratitude and testimony; others note it set Jesus up for confrontation. Whatever the man's motive, his words brought Jesus to the leaders' attention.
verses.memory_phrase: He told them: Jesus healed me.
verses.prayer: Jesus, give me courage to tell others what You have done for me — even when it might cause trouble.
verses.discussion_question: Do you think the man was trying to help Jesus or hurt Him when he told the leaders? Why does your answer matter?
verses.family_discussion: When something amazing happens, do we tell people about Jesus' role in it? How do we share what God has done in our family's life?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone one thing Jesus has done for you or your family — share your testimony!
verses.illustration_prompt: The healed man speaking earnestly to a group of attentive but stern Jewish leaders, pointing in the direction Jesus had gone. His joy is real but the leaders' expressions are darkening.

verses.kjv: And therefore did the Jews persecute Jesus, and sought to slay him, because he had done these things on the sabbath day.
verses.little_bible: Because Jesus healed on the Sabbath, the Jewish leaders started to persecute Him and even began plotting to kill Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The leaders started to persecute Jesus because He healed on the Sabbath.
verses.meaning: A miracle of healing triggers a murderous response. The Sabbath breaking was the legal charge, but the deeper issue was power and authority. A man who could heal with a word was a threat to their position.
verses.memory_phrase: They persecuted Jesus for doing good.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You did good and were treated badly for it. Help me not to be surprised when doing right is hard.
verses.discussion_question: The leaders wanted to kill Jesus for healing someone. How does that make you feel about them? About Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Sometimes doing the right thing causes opposition. Has your family ever faced pushback for doing something good? How did you handle it?
verses.do_it_today: Keep doing what is right today — even if someone questions or criticises you for it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A council of Jewish leaders gathered around a table with intense, angry expressions. Scrolls open before them. One gestures toward the door where Jesus had gone. A plotting, dangerous atmosphere.

verses.kjv: But Jesus answered them, My Father worketh hitherto, and I work.
verses.little_bible: Jesus answered them: 'My Father is always working, and I am working too.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'My Father always works, and I work too.'
verses.meaning: This is a thunderclap of a statement. Jesus calls God 'My Father' — not 'our Father' — claiming a unique, personal relationship. God does not rest from sustaining creation even on the Sabbath, and Jesus, as God's Son, shares in that ongoing work.
verses.memory_phrase: My Father works, and I work.
verses.prayer: Father God, thank You that You never stop caring for me. You are always working in my life.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus said 'My Father' not 'Our Father.' Why is that a big claim? What does it tell us about who Jesus is?
verses.family_discussion: God is always at work — even when we can't see it. Can you think of times God was working in your family's life even when things looked difficult?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God: 'What are You doing in my life right now?' Spend two minutes being quiet and listening.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing calmly before a crowd of accusing leaders, speaking with peaceful confidence and authority. Behind Him, sunlight pours through an archway — suggesting God's light backing His words.

verses.kjv: Therefore the Jews sought the more to kill him, because he not only had broken the sabbath, but said also that God was his Father, making himself equal with God.
verses.little_bible: This made the leaders even more determined to kill Jesus — not just because of the Sabbath, but because He called God His own Father, which meant He was claiming to be equal with God!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The leaders wanted to kill Jesus because He said God was His Father.
verses.meaning: The leaders understood exactly what Jesus meant. Calling God 'My Father' was a claim to divine equality. This is not a misunderstanding — Jesus was making the claim, and the leaders rightly heard it as such. The question is not whether Jesus said it, but whether it is true.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus claimed to be equal with God.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I believe You are truly God's Son and equal with God. I worship You!
verses.discussion_question: The leaders thought Jesus claiming to be God's Son was wrong. What do you believe about who Jesus is?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus never backed down from His claim to be God's Son. Why is this the most important question anyone can answer?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone today one thing you believe about Jesus — practise putting your faith into words.
verses.illustration_prompt: The faces of the Jewish leaders showing shock, anger and horror as Jesus speaks. Some point at Him accusingly, others whisper to each other. Jesus remains composed.

verses.kjv: Then answered Jesus and said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, The Son can do nothing of himself, but what he seeth the Father do: for what things soever he doeth, these also doeth the Son likewise.
verses.little_bible: Jesus replied: 'I'm telling you the truth — the Son does nothing on His own. He only does what He sees the Father doing. Everything the Father does, the Son does the same way.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'I only do what I see my Father doing.'
verses.meaning: Jesus explains His relationship with the Father: perfect unity and submission. Far from claiming independence, Jesus claims intimate alignment — He watches what the Father does and mirrors it perfectly. This is not weakness but perfect love and unity.
verses.memory_phrase: The Son does what the Father does.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me to watch what You do and follow Your example — just like You watch and follow the Father.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus only did what He saw the Father doing. How can we try to do the same — only do what God shows us?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus is the perfect reflection of God the Father. When we look at Jesus, we see exactly what God is like. What does that mean for how we read about Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Before making a decision today, ask: 'What would Jesus do in this situation?' Let that guide your choice.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus and a shining figure of the Father in parallel postures — the Son mirroring the Father's movement exactly, like a perfect reflection. A visual of deep harmony and love.

verses.kjv: For the Father loveth the Son, and sheweth him all things that himself doeth: and he will shew him greater works than these, that ye may marvel.
verses.little_bible: The Father loves the Son and shows Him everything He is doing. And even greater works than this healing are coming — works that will amaze you!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The Father loves the Son and shows Him everything. Greater things are coming!
verses.meaning: The relationship between Father and Son is defined by love and complete openness. The Father hides nothing from the Son. And greater works — including raising the dead — are on their way.
verses.memory_phrase: The Father loves the Son and shows Him all things.
verses.prayer: Father, thank You for loving Jesus perfectly. Help me to experience Your love through Jesus.
verses.discussion_question: The Father shows the Son everything. What does that tell you about the kind of relationship they have?
verses.family_discussion: The Father-Son relationship of God is marked by love, openness and trust. How can your family grow in those same qualities?
verses.do_it_today: Show someone in your family something you love or something important to you today — share your world with them.
verses.illustration_prompt: A warm, glowing depiction of the Father's hand guiding the Son — open, generous, revealing wonders. Light flowing between them like a living river of love.

verses.kjv: For as the Father raiseth up the dead, and quickeneth them; even so the Son quickeneth whom he will.
verses.little_bible: Just as the Father raises dead people back to life, the Son also gives life to anyone He chooses.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Just like the Father gives life, the Son gives life too.
verses.meaning: Jesus claims the power of resurrection — the ability to give life. This is one of the most exclusive divine attributes, and Jesus says He shares it fully. 'Quickeneth' means 'makes alive.'
verses.memory_phrase: The Son gives life to all He chooses.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You have the power to give life. Give life to everything inside me that feels dead.
verses.discussion_question: Only God has the power to raise the dead. What does it mean that Jesus has that same power?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus gives life — not just physical life but spiritual life. What dead areas in our family's life do we need to ask Jesus to bring back to life?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for one thing that feels hopeless or dead in your life — ask Jesus to bring life to it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus extending His hand over a figure lying still, as a gentle golden light begins to glow around the figure — life returning, like dawn breaking over darkness.

verses.kjv: For the Father judgeth no man, but hath committed all judgment unto the Son:
verses.little_bible: The Father does not judge anyone by Himself — He has given all authority to judge to the Son.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The Father has given all judgment to the Son.
verses.meaning: Jesus is not only the giver of life but also the judge of all. God has chosen to exercise all final judgment through the Son. This makes our response to Jesus the most consequential decision a person will ever make.
verses.memory_phrase: All judgment belongs to the Son.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are the Judge. Help me to live in a way that pleases You, knowing I will stand before You one day.
verses.discussion_question: If Jesus is the one who will judge everything, why does that make it so important to have a good relationship with Him now?
verses.family_discussion: God has given Jesus the role of judge. How does knowing that Jesus — who loves us — is also the judge give us both comfort and a warning?
verses.do_it_today: Think about one choice you will make today. Ask yourself: 'What would Jesus, my judge and my friend, think of this?'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus seated on a shining throne, balanced scales of justice glowing before Him — but His face is not harsh. It is serious, wise, and full of deep care.

verses.kjv: That all men should honour the Son, even as they honour the Father. He that honoureth not the Son honoureth not the Father which hath sent him.
verses.little_bible: The reason is this: God wants everyone to honour the Son just as they honour the Father. If you do not honour the Son, you are not honouring the Father who sent Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Everyone must honour Jesus, just as they honour God the Father.
verses.meaning: Honouring the Father and honouring Jesus cannot be separated. They are one. Any religion that claims to worship God while rejecting Jesus is self-deceived. Genuine worship of God includes honouring Jesus as God's equal.
verses.memory_phrase: Honour the Son as you honour the Father.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I honour You as God's own Son. You deserve all my worship and praise.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus says you cannot truly honour God if you don't honour Jesus. Why do you think that is?
verses.family_discussion: Some people say they believe in God but not in Jesus. What does Jesus say about that in this verse? How can we gently share this truth?
verses.do_it_today: Give Jesus honour today — say a prayer that is specifically to Jesus, telling Him how great He is.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two glowing figures — the Father and the Son — side by side, radiant and majestic. People bowing in equal reverence before both. A visual of unified honour.

verses.kjv: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that heareth my word, and believeth on him that sent me, hath everlasting life, and shall not come into condemnation; but is passed from death unto life.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'I'm telling you the truth — whoever hears what I say and trusts in the Father who sent Me already has eternal life. They will not be judged for their sins. They have already crossed over from death into life!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: whoever believes in Him already has eternal life and has crossed from death to life.
verses.meaning: This is the heart of the Gospel in a single verse. Eternal life is not a future reward only — it is a present possession for those who believe. The believer has already passed through death into life. Judgment — condemnation — has no claim on them.
verses.memory_phrase: Believe in Jesus and have eternal life.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I hear Your words and I believe in You. Thank You that I have already passed from death to life!
verses.discussion_question: Jesus says that those who believe in Him have 'already' passed from death to life — right now, not just in the future. How does that make you feel?
verses.family_discussion: Eternal life is not just about what happens after we die — it begins now when we believe in Jesus. How does knowing you already have eternal life change how you live today?
verses.do_it_today: Write down or say out loud: 'I believe in Jesus. I have passed from death to life.' Say it like you mean it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child stepping across a threshold from a dark, grey world into a bright, living garden — sunlight, colour, and joy on the other side. A visual of the passage from death to life.

verses.kjv: Verily, verily, I say unto you, The hour is coming, and now is, when the dead shall hear the voice of the Son of God: and they that hear shall live.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'The time is coming — and it is already here — when the dead will hear the voice of God's Son. And all who hear it will live!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said the dead will hear His voice and live.
verses.meaning: Jesus speaks of both spiritual resurrection now (the spiritually dead hearing the gospel and coming to life) and physical resurrection to come. His voice has power over death in both senses.
verses.memory_phrase: The dead will hear His voice and live.
verses.prayer: Jesus, speak to the parts of my heart that feel dead. I want to hear Your voice and truly live!
verses.discussion_question: How can someone who is spiritually dead hear Jesus' voice? What does it look like when that happens?
verses.family_discussion: Have any of you experienced a moment when you first really heard Jesus' voice — when something clicked and came alive? Share those stories.
verses.do_it_today: Spend five minutes reading or listening to Jesus' words today — not just reading quickly, but really listening.
verses.illustration_prompt: A hushed moment: Jesus speaking, and a crowd below Him gradually lifting their heads, wide-eyed, as if waking from a deep sleep — spiritual life returning to their faces.

verses.kjv: For as the Father hath life in himself; so hath he given to the Son to have life in himself;
verses.little_bible: Just as the Father has life in Himself, He has also given the Son to have life within Himself.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The Father has life in Himself, and He gave the Son life in Himself too.
verses.meaning: God is the source of all life — He doesn't receive life, He is the fountain of it. And He has granted the Son to share this same self-existing life. Jesus is not just alive — He is Life itself.
verses.memory_phrase: The Father has life; He gave life to the Son.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are Life itself. Let Your life flow into me and fill everything I do.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between having life and being the source of life? Why does it matter that Jesus is the source?
verses.family_discussion: Everything alive depends on God for its life. How does that help us understand our dependence on God each day?
verses.do_it_today: Every time you breathe today, remember: God is the one who gives you life. Thank Him for it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A radiant flowing spring of pure light — from the Father to the Son — representing life-source passing between them. The same living light, freely given.

verses.kjv: And hath given him authority to execute judgment also, because he is the Son of man.
verses.little_bible: And God gave Him authority to judge because He is the Son of Man.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God gave Jesus authority to judge because He became human.
verses.meaning: 'Son of Man' is Jesus' favourite way to refer to Himself — it is the title from Daniel 7 for the divine figure who comes to receive all dominion and glory. Jesus is qualified to judge humanity because He became human — He understands from the inside.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus has authority to judge.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You became human to understand me. Thank You for being a judge who truly knows what it is like to be me.
verses.discussion_question: Why might it matter that the one who judges us has also been a human being? How does that make Jesus different from a distant judge?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus experienced hunger, tiredness, loss, and temptation — yet without sin. How does that help us trust His judgments about our lives?
verses.do_it_today: Share a problem you're facing with Jesus — knowing He truly understands human struggle.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing in two realms at once — robed in divine glory but with the face and hands of a real, human person. Both fully God and fully man.

verses.kjv: Marvel not at this: for the hour is coming, in the which all that are in the graves shall hear his voice,
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Don't be amazed by this — for the hour is coming when everyone who has ever died will hear His voice!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A time is coming when everyone in their graves will hear Jesus' voice.
verses.meaning: Jesus points ahead to the final resurrection — not just spiritual resurrection now, but physical resurrection at the last day when every person who has ever lived will hear His voice. This is a universal event.
verses.memory_phrase: Everyone in their graves will hear His voice.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You have power over death and the grave. I am not afraid of death because You have conquered it.
verses.discussion_question: Why would it be amazing that everyone who has ever died will one day hear Jesus' voice? What does that say about Jesus' power?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus' power over death is the foundation of our hope. How does believing in the resurrection change the way we think about loved ones who have died?
verses.do_it_today: Talk about one person your family has loved who is now with God. Thank Jesus that His voice will raise them one day.
verses.illustration_prompt: Graves in a quiet hillside at dawn, the first rays of light touching the earth — a sense of something powerful about to happen, of a voice about to break through the silence.

verses.kjv: And shall come forth; they that have done good, unto the resurrection of life; and they that have done evil, unto the resurrection of damnation.
verses.little_bible: And they will come out — those who have lived for God will rise to life, but those who have rejected God will rise to face judgment.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Those who did good will rise to life; those who did evil will face judgment.
verses.meaning: The resurrection has two outcomes. This is not salvation by works — 'done good' and 'done evil' summarise the directions of one's life that flow from belief or unbelief. The reference is to the kind of life that flows from faith in Christ.
verses.memory_phrase: Rise to life or rise to judgment.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I want to live a life that pleases You. Help me to do good because I love You.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it important to know that every person will face judgment one day? How should that affect the way we live?
verses.family_discussion: Judgment is not a comfortable topic, but Jesus spoke about it clearly because He loves us and wants us to be ready. How do we talk about this honestly and with hope?
verses.do_it_today: Choose one thing to do today that is genuinely good — an act of kindness, generosity, or truth-telling.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two paths diverging from a single gate — one bathed in warm golden light leading upward, one leading into shadow. Both real, both certain. A moment of sober clarity.

verses.kjv: I can of mine own self do nothing: as I hear, I judge: and my judgment is just; because I seek not mine own will, but the will of the Father which hath sent me.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'I can do nothing by My own authority. I judge according to what the Father tells Me. My judgment is fair because I am not trying to do what I want — I am doing what the Father wants.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'I do the Father's will, not my own — so my judgment is always right.'
verses.meaning: Jesus' judgments are perfectly just because they are perfectly aligned with the Father's will. He has no self-interest, no bias, no agenda. This is what makes Him the perfect judge.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus seeks the Father's will, not His own.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me to seek God's will rather than my own — just like You did.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus says His judgment is fair because He seeks the Father's will, not His own. What makes a person's decisions unfair? What makes them fair?
verses.family_discussion: When we seek our own will over God's will, our decisions get distorted. How can your family build the habit of seeking God's will first in decisions?
verses.do_it_today: Before making one decision today, stop and ask: 'What does God want here?' Then do that.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus in prayer, hands open toward heaven in a posture of complete surrender — seeking the Father's will. Light descending on Him like an approving blessing.

verses.kjv: If I bear witness of myself, my witness is not true.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'If I were the only one saying these things about Myself, you might not believe Me.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'A person cannot be the only witness for themselves.'
verses.meaning: Under Jewish law, a person's testimony about themselves did not count as valid proof. Jesus acknowledges this principle and prepares to call other witnesses to establish who He is.
verses.memory_phrase: One witness alone is not enough.
verses.prayer: Lord, thank You that You provide many witnesses so we can be sure. Help my faith to be grounded in evidence.
verses.discussion_question: Why do we usually need more than one person to confirm an important truth? Can you think of examples from everyday life?
verses.family_discussion: God has given us many witnesses to confirm who Jesus is — the Bible, changed lives, history. How has God confirmed Jesus' identity to your family?
verses.do_it_today: Find one piece of evidence outside the Bible — a historical fact, a changed life, a fulfilled prophecy — that confirms something about Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: A Jewish courtroom scene with scrolls and a judge, showing that testimony requires multiple witnesses. Jesus standing calmly, about to present His evidence.

verses.kjv: There is another that beareth witness of me; and I know that the witness which he witnesseth of me is true.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'But there is another witness who speaks up for Me — and I know His testimony about Me is completely true.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'There is another witness who tells the truth about Me.'
verses.meaning: Jesus refers to the Father, whose witness is perfect and cannot be doubted. The Father testifies to the Son through His works, His voice, and ultimately through the Scriptures.
verses.memory_phrase: Another witness testifies for Jesus.
verses.prayer: Father, thank You that You speak up for Jesus. Help me to trust what You say about Him.
verses.discussion_question: Who is the 'another' Jesus talks about? Why is that witness the most reliable of all?
verses.family_discussion: God the Father testifies about Jesus. Where do we hear the Father's voice today — how does He still speak up for Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Read one Old Testament prophecy that points to Jesus (like Isaiah 53 or Micah 5:2). Notice God the Father pointing to His Son.
verses.illustration_prompt: A radiant, unseen presence behind Jesus — suggested by light and warmth — testifying on His behalf. Jesus gestures toward this presence with confidence.

verses.kjv: Ye sent unto John, and he bare witness unto the truth.
verses.little_bible: Jesus reminded them: 'You sent people to John the Baptist, and he told you the truth about Me.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John the Baptist told the truth about Jesus.
verses.meaning: Jesus calls His first human witness: John the Baptist. The leaders themselves had sent a delegation to John, and John had clearly testified that Jesus was the Lamb of God. They had the testimony — they chose not to accept it.
verses.memory_phrase: John the Baptist testified about Jesus.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me to be like John — someone who points people to Jesus honestly and boldly.
verses.discussion_question: The leaders had already heard John the Baptist speak about Jesus. Why might they have chosen to ignore what John said?
verses.family_discussion: John the Baptist's whole life was pointing people to Jesus. Who has God used to point your family to Jesus? Give thanks for them.
verses.do_it_today: Think of one person God used to lead you toward Jesus. Send them a message or write them a note of thanks.
verses.illustration_prompt: John the Baptist in the Jordan, arm extended and pointing toward the horizon where Jesus walks — a bold, clear witness. The image of a life devoted to pointing away from self toward Jesus.

verses.kjv: But I receive not testimony from man: but these things I say, that ye might be saved.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'I don't actually need a man to confirm who I am — but I'm telling you all this so that you can be saved!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'I tell you these things so that you will be saved.'
verses.meaning: Jesus does not depend on human validation — He is God. But He brings up these witnesses because He is passionate about the salvation of His listeners. His words come from love, not defensiveness.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus speaks so we can be saved.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that everything You say to us is because You want us to be saved. I want to be saved!
verses.discussion_question: Jesus says He tells people these things so they can be saved. How does knowing that Jesus' words come from love change how you receive them?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus is not trying to win an argument — He is trying to save people. How does that motivation shape the way we should share the gospel with others?
verses.do_it_today: Share something you believe about Jesus with someone today — not to win an argument, but because you want them to know the truth.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus speaking to a crowd with open hands — not a defensive posture but an inviting one. His expression full of love and longing for them to receive the truth.

verses.kjv: He was a burning and a shining light: and ye were willing for a season to rejoice in his light.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said about John: 'He was a bright, burning lamp, and for a while you were happy to enjoy his light.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: John was a bright light, and people were glad for a while.
verses.meaning: John the Baptist was a lamp — a wonderful, bright witness. But a lamp is not the sun. People were drawn to John's light, but only for a season. Their interest was superficial, not transformative.
verses.memory_phrase: John was a bright burning lamp.
verses.prayer: God, help me to be a light for You — not for my own glory, but to point people to Jesus, the true Light.
verses.discussion_question: Why might people have enjoyed John's light 'for a season' but not followed what he said about Jesus? What did they like about him without truly accepting his message?
verses.family_discussion: It is possible to be interested in Christian things without truly following Jesus. What is the difference between enjoying the light and living in it?
verses.do_it_today: Shine some light today — encourage someone, share truth, or do something kind that reflects Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: A glowing, burning lamp in the darkness — warm and beautiful, drawing moths and people toward it. John stands beside it, pointing beyond it to an even greater light on the horizon.

verses.kjv: But I have greater witness than that of John: for the works which the Father hath given me to finish, the same works that I do, bear witness of me, that the Father hath sent me.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'But I have an even greater witness than John — the miracles and works the Father gave Me to do. These works show that the Father sent Me.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus' miracles are an even greater witness than John's words.
verses.meaning: Jesus' second witness is His works — the miracles themselves. The healing at Bethesda, the turning of water to wine, and all the miracles are God's own testimony that Jesus is the one He sent.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus' works prove who He is.
verses.prayer: Jesus, Your works show that You are the Son of God. I believe in You because of who You are and what You do.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus says His miracles are proof of who He is. Can you name three miracles of Jesus that 'witness' to His identity?
verses.family_discussion: God does works in our lives too — things that point to His reality and love. What works has God done in your family that prove He is real?
verses.do_it_today: Write down three things God has done in your life or family. These are His 'works' — His witnesses to His love.
verses.illustration_prompt: A montage of miracles: healing, feeding, calming the storm — all glowing with divine energy. Each one a testimony pointing to Jesus as God's sent Son.

verses.kjv: And the Father himself, which hath sent me, hath borne witness of me. Ye have neither heard his voice at any time, nor seen his shape.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'The Father Himself — who sent Me — has testified about Me. But you have never heard His voice or seen what He looks like.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The Father Himself has testified about Jesus.
verses.meaning: The Father's testimony came at Jesus' baptism and transfiguration. But the leaders, despite all their religious devotion, had never actually encountered the living God. Their religion was about texts and traditions, not relationship.
verses.memory_phrase: The Father Himself testifies for Jesus.
verses.prayer: Father God, let me truly know You — not just know about You. Help me hear Your voice.
verses.discussion_question: Is it possible to be very religious without ever truly knowing God? What is the difference?
verses.family_discussion: The leaders knew a lot about God but did not know God Himself. How do we make sure our faith is a real relationship, not just religious activity?
verses.do_it_today: Spend time not just reading about God but actually talking to Him — have a real conversation in prayer.
verses.illustration_prompt: The leaders surrounded by scrolls and texts — all the knowledge about God — but in the background, a shining presence that they are not looking at. So close to God, yet so far.

verses.kjv: And ye have not his word abiding in you: for whom he hath sent, him ye believe not.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'God's word does not live inside you — if it did, you would believe in Me, because the Father sent Me.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God's word was not living in their hearts.
verses.meaning: The test of whether God's word truly lives in a person is whether they receive Jesus. Rejecting Jesus while claiming to follow God's word is a contradiction — because the Scriptures point to Jesus.
verses.memory_phrase: If God's word lives in you, you will believe in Jesus.
verses.prayer: God, let Your Word truly live inside my heart — not just in my head but deep inside me.
verses.discussion_question: How can someone read the Bible every day but still not have God's word 'living inside' them? What is the difference?
verses.family_discussion: Reading the Bible is valuable — but the Word needs to actually change us and lead us to Jesus. How do we read it in a way that lets it live in us?
verses.do_it_today: Read one verse today and ask: 'How does this point me to Jesus?' Let it live in you.
verses.illustration_prompt: A beautiful scroll glowing with golden light — but held at arm's length, not taken in. The word on the outside, not the inside. A picture of missed transformation.

verses.kjv: Search the scriptures; for in them ye think ye have eternal life: and they are they which testify of me.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'You search the Scriptures because you think they will give you eternal life. But these very Scriptures are pointing to Me!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The Scriptures point to Jesus — they are about Him!
verses.meaning: This is one of the most important hermeneutical statements in the Bible. The whole Old Testament is a testimony to Jesus. To read the Bible and miss Jesus is to miss the point entirely.
verses.memory_phrase: The Scriptures all point to Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me to read the Bible and always find You there — because it is all about You!
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of any Old Testament stories that point to Jesus? How do they connect to Him?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus says the whole Bible points to Him. How does that change the way you read the Old Testament? Try to find Jesus in a familiar Old Testament story.
verses.do_it_today: Read a short Old Testament story together and ask: 'How does this point to Jesus?' (Try: the Passover lamb, or Moses lifting up the snake.)
verses.illustration_prompt: An open scroll filled with Hebrew text — and embedded in the words, glowing faintly, is the outline of a cross. The Word pointing to the Word.

verses.kjv: And ye will not come to me, that ye might have life.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said sadly: 'But you will not come to Me so that you can have life.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They would not come to Jesus to receive life.
verses.meaning: The problem is not lack of information — they have the Scriptures and the witnesses. The problem is unwillingness. 'Ye will not' is a statement of stubborn resistance. Life is available; they choose not to come.
verses.memory_phrase: They would not come to Jesus for life.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I choose to come to You today. I don't want to be stubborn — I want You and the life You give.
verses.discussion_question: Why would someone refuse to come to Jesus when He offers them life? What gets in the way?
verses.family_discussion: Pride, love of comfort, fear of change — these are common reasons people resist coming to Jesus. Have any of those ever held you back? What helped you come to Him anyway?
verses.do_it_today: Come to Jesus today — right now, just in your heart. Say: 'Jesus, I come to You.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A glowing door of light, wide open and inviting. A figure stands at a distance, arms crossed, face turned slightly away. The door waits — patient and open.

verses.kjv: I receive not honour from men.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'I do not need people to praise or honour Me.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus did not need people's praise.
verses.meaning: Jesus is not motivated by human approval. His identity is not dependent on what people think of Him. This is in stark contrast to the leaders, whose entire lives were shaped by the opinions of others.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus did not need human praise.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me not to be addicted to other people's praise. Help me to find my worth in You.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it a problem to care too much about what other people think of us? How does Jesus model a better way?
verses.family_discussion: We live in a culture obsessed with likes, followers and approval. How do we raise children who find their worth in God rather than in the approval of others?
verses.do_it_today: Do one good thing today just because it is right — not because anyone will see it or praise you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus walking quietly past a cheering crowd, not stopping to receive their applause — His focus entirely on the Father, not on the noise of human praise.

verses.kjv: But I know you, that ye have not the love of God in you.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'But I know you — and you do not have the love of God living inside your hearts.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said, 'The love of God is not inside you.'
verses.meaning: Jesus diagnoses the root problem: not lack of knowledge, not lack of Scriptures, but lack of love for God. All their religious activity was hollow because there was no genuine love for God at the centre.
verses.memory_phrase: They had no love for God inside them.
verses.prayer: God, fill my heart with true love for You — not just religious activity, but real love.
verses.discussion_question: How is it possible to do lots of religious things without actually loving God? What does loving God actually look like?
verses.family_discussion: Religious activity without love for God is like a body without a heart. How does your family keep love for God at the centre of everything?
verses.do_it_today: Tell God you love Him — not out of duty, but sincerely. Then do one thing today that flows from that love.
verses.illustration_prompt: An ornate religious vessel — beautiful on the outside, decorated and polished — but empty inside. A visual of religion without love.

verses.kjv: I am come in my Father's name, and ye receive me not: if another shall come in his own name, him ye will receive.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'I came representing My Father, and you will not receive Me. But if someone comes only representing themselves, you will welcome them!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They rejected Jesus but would welcome false leaders who sought their own glory.
verses.meaning: Jesus came with the Father's authority and mission, asking nothing for Himself. Yet they rejected Him and would readily accept self-promoting leaders. This reveals a preference for human ego over divine humility.
verses.memory_phrase: He came in the Father's name — and was rejected.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I receive You today. You came representing the Father, and I welcome You into my life.
verses.discussion_question: Why might people prefer leaders who are impressive and self-promoting over someone like Jesus, who was humble and honest?
verses.family_discussion: We are all tempted to follow charismatic but self-centred leaders. How do we discern whether a leader is pointing to God or to themselves?
verses.do_it_today: Evaluate one leader or voice in your life: are they pointing you to God or to themselves? What does that tell you?
verses.illustration_prompt: Two figures at a crossroads: Jesus, humble and radiant with the Father's light behind Him — and a grand, self-important figure with no light behind them. The crowd turning toward the wrong one.

verses.kjv: How can ye believe, which receive honour one of another, and seek not the honour that cometh from God only?
verses.little_bible: Jesus asked them: 'How can you possibly believe, when you spend all your time trying to impress each other — instead of seeking the honour that only comes from God?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They cared too much about impressing each other to truly believe in Jesus.
verses.meaning: Craving human approval is a direct obstacle to faith. You cannot simultaneously be fixated on what people think of you and truly believe in Jesus, who calls you away from that entirely.
verses.memory_phrase: You can't seek human praise and truly believe.
verses.prayer: God, help me to care about Your honour and praise more than what other people think of me.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of a time when you didn't do the right thing because you were worried about what your friends would think? How does that relate to this verse?
verses.family_discussion: Peer pressure is not just a child's problem. How does the desire for human approval limit your family's faith and obedience to God?
verses.do_it_today: Choose one thing today you know is right, even if you're not sure everyone will approve. Do it for God's honour.
verses.illustration_prompt: A group of elaborately dressed leaders bowing to each other in a circle, admiring each other's robes and titles — all eyes turned inward, none looking up toward God.

verses.kjv: Do not think that I will accuse you to the Father: there is one that accuseth you, even Moses, in whom ye trust.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Don't think I will be the one to accuse you before the Father — Moses himself will accuse you! And he is the one you say you trust!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said Moses himself will accuse them, not Jesus.
verses.meaning: Jesus flips their accusation on its head. They think they stand on Moses' authority against Jesus. But Moses himself will be their accuser — because they have not truly followed what Moses wrote.
verses.memory_phrase: Moses will accuse those who reject Jesus.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me to be consistent — to truly live by the things I say I believe.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it ironic that the leaders trusted Moses but rejected Jesus? What had Moses actually written?
verses.family_discussion: It is possible to claim to follow something while actually contradicting it. Where might we be inconsistent between what we say we believe and how we actually live?
verses.do_it_today: Choose one thing you say you believe and find one way to live it out more consistently today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Moses holding the stone tablets with an expression of serious accusation — directed not at Jesus but at the Jewish leaders who claim to follow him. A profound irony made visual.

verses.kjv: For had ye believed Moses, ye would have believed me: for he wrote of me.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'If you had truly believed what Moses wrote, you would believe in Me — because he wrote about Me!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Moses wrote about Jesus — if they believed Moses, they would believe Jesus.
verses.meaning: The Law, the Pentateuch — the five books of Moses — point to Jesus. The sacrificial system, the Passover lamb, the manna, the bronze serpent, the promised prophet — all of it was testimony to Jesus.
verses.memory_phrase: Moses wrote about Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, open my eyes to see You in all of the Bible — even the oldest parts.
verses.discussion_question: Moses wrote about Jesus? Can you think of something Moses wrote that could be pointing to Jesus? (Hint: Passover lamb, the prophet to come...)
verses.family_discussion: Read Deuteronomy 18:15-18 together — Moses' prophecy of 'a prophet like me.' Who is that prophet? How did Jesus fulfil it?
verses.do_it_today: Read one Old Testament passage together and find Jesus in it. Make it a treasure hunt for Jesus in the Old Testament.
verses.illustration_prompt: Moses writing on a scroll, and embedded in the words being written is a gentle glow — the shape of a cross, a lamb, a light. The pen of Moses writing about Jesus without fully knowing it.

verses.kjv: But if ye believe not his writings, how shall ye believe my words?
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'But if you do not believe what Moses wrote, how will you ever believe what I say?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: If they wouldn't believe Moses, they wouldn't believe Jesus either.
verses.meaning: Jesus closes with a powerful logical challenge. The foundation of belief in Jesus is laid in the Old Testament. Those who reject the Scriptures have already closed the door to receiving Jesus' words.
verses.memory_phrase: If you don't believe Moses, you won't believe Me.
verses.prayer: God, give me a heart that truly believes Your whole Word — from beginning to end.
verses.discussion_question: Why does not believing the Old Testament make it harder to believe Jesus? How are they connected?
verses.family_discussion: The whole Bible is one connected story pointing to Jesus. How does your family read and value the Old Testament as part of that story?
verses.do_it_today: Read one Old Testament and one New Testament passage side by side today — and look for the connection between them.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two open books side by side — the Old Testament scroll and the New Testament — with a golden thread connecting them, showing they tell one story. Jesus stands where the thread meets.
